enriched_points.expanded_content: Recent advancements in all-atom and coarse-grained force fields enhance the modeling of protein folding, allowing for accurate predictions of structural conformations and dynamics.
Innovative methodologies focus on simulating transient intermediates during folding, providing insights into the kinetics and pathways that proteins undergo during their conformational changes.
Continuous optimization of force fields, including torsion potentials and water models, significantly boosts the accuracy of simulations, facilitating the study of complex folding events.
